verdict.answer_first: Both answer immediately: A says "It's an infinite loop" and B says "It's an infinite loop" in sentence 1; B is cleaner without the cutesy emoji.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more clearly: "The integer-counter version is better"; A surveys "roughly in order of preference" and includes a weak third option.
verdict.fabrication: B has the worse unsupported/incorrect detail: "until x overflows to Infinity"; A also has a questionable aside, "around 2^53," but B's claim is more plainly wrong for this loop behavior.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the two practical fixes and names the preferred one; A adds an epsilon option that is not a good fix as written.
